renovated_since_last_survey: This is my first survey for Pecan Street (or firs survey at new house)
renovation_desc: In 2018, the home was significantly remodeled, including a second story addition of 847 sq ft and a kitchen remodel. The second story addition is very well insulated (and has good double-pane windows) and does not have a central heating or cooling system. The project also upgraded the thermal performance of the existing shell including insulating the front entrance area, replacing the front door, rebuilding the back of the house (modern construction with good insulation and double-pane windows), and insulating the crawlspace.   In 2005, the core part of the house that was not part of the 2018 remodel replaced windows with double pane, insulated walls, and insulated the attic.  The 2018 remodel also electrified the home. The gas forced air furnace serving the downstairs was replaced with an air handler. Heat is provided by a hot water coil drawing from a new combined space and water heating heat pump system. Gas range was replaced with electric ovens and an induction range. Gas water heating was eliminated. We already had a heat pump dryer. Level 2 EV charging and a solar array (sized for zero net energy) was added.  At this point, the entire home is electric, solarized, and has good efficiency characteristics.  Note that in question 3, you do not have an HVAC system that accurately describes what we have. We have a Sanden heat pump water heating system that creates water in a 120-gallon tank. This is not a split system: the evaporator and condenser are in one exterior unit. The system is not ductless: heat is transferred to an air handler through a coil that distributes heated air in ducts. The system is not two-way: it only provides heating. I will choose two-way heat pump slit system and no air conditioning as perhaps the best answer.  Note that in question 4, we do not have any air conditioner compressors. We have a heat pump that makes hot water.  I just didn't answer this one.  Note in question 47, I marked that I do not own a home energy storage system. But I do have storage for the energy used for space heating in our combined space and water heating system.

renovation_desc: I hope I'm not messing up your responses, but the house was built in 2017 and has not been *upgraded* in the last year, but it was built as a super-efficient house meant to be run entirely off our rooftop solar.  It has triple-pane windows, R40 walls, R60 roof and it's very tightly sealed.
thermostat_brand: We don't have real thermostats.  We just have remote controls for our Mitsubishi heat pumps.
thermostat_is_programmable: I don't have a programmable thermostat
other_electronic_device_freq_used: To clarify, the cable box I specified above is for internet use only; I'm not sure if that makes a difference. We haven't touched it since the day it was first plugged in and connected to our router. There is a heater for a 10 gallon fish tank that is always plugged in and turns on when the water gets below 73 degrees F or so.  We leave a small LED light on in the basement at all times so the cats can see properly to use their litter box, but we are getting a basement door installed soon and it will have a window, so we might not need the light any more.  Also, we have 2 digital clocks, one of which has a small light inside it.
pv_system_reason: It's part of our goal to do as much as possible to avert climate disaster.
pv_system_features_liked: The house was designed to be ideal for solar.  We are about 200 feet from any trees, on a hill, facing due south, with a 12/10 pitch roof.  It's perfect for solar.  I also like that we got an inverter that allows us to use about 2 kW of power even if the grid is down, as long as the sun is shining.
pv_system_features_like_to_improve: We just need about 2kW more to get all the way to net zero - we might be adding that this year.
pv_system_questions_asked: People always ask us how much it cost.
pv_system_surprising_features: People are really shocked to learn that a solar array that doesn't even completely cover our roof is enough to generate almost all the electricity we need for a year even though we live in rainy, cloudy Ithaca NY! Lots of people think solar only works well if you live in Arizona.
ev_buy_or_lease_again_reason: Again, part of our plan to do whatever we can to avert climate disaster and be a part of the transition away from fossil fuels.

other_electronic_device_freq_used: Difficult to answer question 10.  We have a TiVo that is both a DVR and the Cable box.  We have 3 TiVi minis that augment the system in other rooms.
pv_system_reason: In two phases.  The first 6 panel system was to eliminate the highest tier of electric pricing and seemed to be the smartest $$.  Later we leased an electric car and added the additional 18 panels.
pv_system_features_liked: The ability to charge 2 electric cars and use the air conditioning without worry.  Knowing that our carbon impact is minimal adds to our enjoyment. An unexpected benefit was the reduction in needed air conditioning because of the shade on the roof from the solar panels.
pv_system_features_like_to_improve: Our fist system is on a string inverter and the second uses micro inverters.  I have to use two different systems to monitor them.  I wish they were combined.
pv_system_questions_asked: Mostly about the cost.  It's a tough question to answer.  It's less of a cost than an investment.
pv_system_surprising_features: That it's silly to buy more production than you'll need.  The cost benefit is not there.  Only to do it if you want to be uber green.
pv_pos_rate_increase_protection: Protection against future utility rate increases
ev_not_buy_or_lease_again_reason: Not planning to buy another car for some time.  I use public transit to go to work, my wife drives the Tesla, my daughter has a newer car.
how_long_irrigation_sys_run_2: Rachio internet connected irrigation controller waters as needed.
